steps.title: Identify the grammatical relationships
steps.content: The left side has a plural subject scientists. The right side begins with for evidence, which is a participial phrase modifying the action of the subject. The blank requires a past-tense verb to maintain tense agreement with the context of a recent study (a completed action).

steps.title: Apply the correct verb form
steps.content: The correct verb must be singular-plural agreeable and in simple past tense: A) searched. Choices B and D violate subject-verb agreement (B is a participle, D is present perfect) or tense alignment with recent study. That matches choice A — A is correct.
